author: Process Engineer, BHP (WA)
report: Started using Claude to interpret HYSYS simulation warnings. It correctly identified a heat exchanger pinch point I'd missed in a copper concentrator flowsheet. Saved about 2 hours of manual checking. Won't replace running the sim, but good as a second pair of eyes.

author: Graduate Engineer, Woodside (Karratha)
report: Used GPT-5.4 to draft HAZOP guidewords for a new LNG train modification. It generated 80% of the scenarios correctly but missed two critical failure modes related to cryogenic embrittlement. Senior engineer caught them. Good for first-pass brainstorming, not for final safety assessment.

author: Lab Manager, CSL (Broadmeadows)
report: Built a simple ML model predicting batch yield from 12 process parameters. 89% accuracy on validation set. Management is impressed but regulatory team wants to understand how to validate it under GMP. The tech works, the compliance pathway is the bottleneck.

author: Water Treatment Engineer, SA Water
report: Deployed a basic anomaly detection model on our membrane filtration data. Caught a fouling event 6 hours before the pressure differential alarmed. Small win but proved the concept to the ops team. Now pushing for a proper digital twin pilot.

author: ChemE Student, University of Melbourne
report: Using Claude Code to write Python scripts for my capstone project — modelling a CO2 capture process. It wrote the mass balance equations faster than I could in MATLAB. Supervisor is cautiously supportive but wants me to verify every output manually. Fair enough.

author: Environmental Engineer, Orica (Melbourne)
report: Tried using ChatGPT to draft a National Pollutant Inventory (NPI) report. It understood the format but hallucinated two emission factors. If I hadn't checked, we'd have submitted incorrect data to the regulator. Useful for structure, dangerous for numbers.
